evaluation_criteria.quality_of_coverage: How comprehensively the tutorial covers Rust topics from basics to advanced concepts
evaluation_criteria.content: Quality of explanations, clarity, organization, and usefulness for JS/TS developers
evaluation_criteria.accuracy: Technical correctness of Rust concepts, syntax, and best practices
evaluation_criteria.examples: Quality, quantity, and relevance of code examples provided

evaluation_summary.scoring_scale: 1-10, where 1 is low and 10 is high

notes.quality_of_coverage: Covers essential Rust topics well including ownership, borrowing, structs, enums, error handling, traits, generics, and modules. Missing some advanced topics like async/await, smart pointers, and concurrency in depth.
notes.content: Well-structured with clear JS/TS analogies. Good progression from basics to practical project. Clear explanations throughout.
notes.accuracy: Technically accurate with correct Rust syntax and concepts. Proper explanation of ownership model and borrow checker.
notes.examples: Good code examples throughout with practical comparisons to JS/TS. Includes a complete guessing game project.

notes.quality_of_coverage: Most comprehensive tutorial covering all aspects from basics to advanced topics including async/await, smart pointers (Box, Rc, RefCell), concurrency, threads, message passing, mutex, testing, and ecosystem tools.
notes.content: Exceptional organization with clear sections, comparison tables, exercises with solutions, and best practices. Excellent JS/TS analogies throughout.
notes.accuracy: Technically precise with correct syntax, patterns, and Rust idioms. Proper handling of lifetimes, error types, and advanced concepts.
notes.examples: Rich code examples for every concept, including exercises with solutions. Covers unit tests, integration tests, async patterns, and real-world API examples.

notes.quality_of_coverage: Excellent coverage including fundamentals, ownership, structs, enums, error handling, collections, concurrency, and async/await. Includes project-based learning section with beginner to advanced projects.
notes.content: Well-organized with clear explanations and good JS/TS comparisons. Includes structured learning path (weeks 1-12) and common pitfalls section.
notes.accuracy: Technically accurate with proper Rust concepts. Good explanation of ownership model and error handling patterns.
notes.examples: Good examples throughout with practical project suggestions. Could have more inline code examples for some concepts.

notes.quality_of_coverage: Good coverage of core concepts including ownership, borrowing, error handling, traits, modules, and testing. Some advanced topics like async and smart pointers could be more detailed.
notes.content: Well-structured with good JS/TS comparisons. Includes summary checklist and common pitfalls section. Some sections could be more detailed.
notes.accuracy: Generally accurate with correct Rust syntax. Some minor issues in code examples (e.g., incomplete function implementations).
notes.examples: Decent examples but some are incomplete or have syntax issues. Could benefit from more complete working examples.

notes.quality_of_coverage: Comprehensive coverage of fundamentals through advanced topics including ownership, structs, enums, error handling, collections, concurrency, generics, traits, and testing. Includes TODO API project.
notes.content: Well-organized with clear sections and good JS/TS comparisons. Good transition guide section for JS/TS developers.
notes.accuracy: Technically accurate with correct Rust patterns and best practices. Proper explanation of ownership and borrowing rules.
notes.examples: Good examples throughout including a complete TODO API project. Some sections could use more detailed examples.

notes.quality_of_coverage: Covers essential topics including installation, variables, functions, ownership, borrowing, structs, enums, error handling, collections, and concurrency. More concise than other tutorials.
notes.content: Concise and practical with good JS/TS analogies. Includes exercises throughout. Could benefit from more detailed explanations in some areas.
notes.accuracy: Technically accurate with correct Rust concepts. Good explanation of ownership model and borrow checker.
notes.examples: Good practical examples with exercises. More concise than other tutorials but still provides working code.

recommendations.best_overall: rust-tutorial-qwen3.5-27b.md - Most comprehensive with excellent examples and accuracy
recommendations.best_for_beginners: rust-tutorial-grok4.1Fast.md - Clear explanations with good JS/TS analogies
recommendations.best_project_based: rust-tutorial-mistral-large.md - Includes structured learning path and project ideas
recommendations.most_concise: rust-tutorial-misral-large.md - Quick reference with essential concepts
